description: Comprehensive optimization of learn:profile and learn:plan commands based on 5-round multi-CLI analysis to address trust gaps, architectural debt, and intelligence enhancement
approach: Trust-First Evolution Strategy: Prioritize evidence trustworthiness and QA before architectural refactoring. Critical path: Real MCP verification → Evidence structure → Schema validation → DAG detection → Agent-based planning. Maintain Isolated Strategy with zero core modifications.

tasks.title: Implement Real MCP Verification Framework
tasks.description: Replace simulated micro-challenge validation with real code execution and test verification to establish objective evidence-based assessment

modification_points.change: Replace AskUserQuestion self-report with code capture and mcp-runner.js execution
Create isolated verification script for executing user code against test cases

implementation: Create .claude/commands/learn/_internal/mcp-runner.js with Node.js child process execution
Implement test case format and fixture loading mechanism
Modify challenge flow in profile.md to prompt for code submission (paste or file)
Add Bash tool invocation: node mcp-runner.js <code-file> <test-file>
Parse JSON result: {tests_passed, tests_total, score, execution_time}
Update challengeScore calculation based on real test results
Store evidence with type: real_mcp and verification metadata

unit: Test timeout handling and error cases
integration: Run full profile assessment with code submission
Verify evidence array contains verification metadata
Check confidence scores match evidence types

criteria: User code is captured and stored in evidence array
Tests execute in isolated environment (no file/network access)
Proficiency scores reflect actual test pass rates
Confidence values distinguish verified vs self-reported evidence
Security: No eval(), restricted child process permissions
verification: Submit correct code → high proficiency score
Submit incorrect code → low proficiency score

commit.message_template: implement real MCP verification framework for objective skill assessment

tasks.title: Strengthen Evidence Data Structure
tasks.description: Enhance evidence field structure to support verification provenance and confidence calibration with explicit evidence types

modification_points.change: Change from string array to object array with type and metadata
Update to populate new evidence structure with type and verification_metadata

implementation: Update profile schema: evidence array items from string to object
Add evidence_type enum: self-report | conceptual | tool-verified
Add verification_metadata object: {method, timestamp, test_results, confidence_source}
Modify confidence calculation rules based on evidence_type
Implement confidence caps: self-report ≤ 0.5, conceptual ≤ 0.7, tool-verified ≤ 0.95
Update profile.md evidence.push() calls to use new structure
Add migration logic for existing profiles (graceful degradation)

unit: Test backward compatibility with old evidence format
integration: Create profile with mixed evidence types
Load old profile → verify graceful handling

criteria: Evidence objects contain type and verification_metadata
Confidence scores respect evidence-type caps
Profile schema validates evidence structure
Existing profiles remain compatible (graceful degradation)
Documentation updated with new evidence format
verification: Schema validation passes for new profiles
Confidence caps are enforced in calculations

commit.message_template: strengthen evidence data structure with verification provenance

tasks.title: Fix Schema Path Inconsistencies
tasks.description: Unify all schema path references to single canonical location to eliminate validation failures
implementation: Audit all schema references: grep -r learn.*schema.json .claude/
Establish canonical location: .claude/workflows/cli-templates/schemas/
Update plan.md schema loading to use canonical path
Update learn-planning-agent.md schema references
Add schema path validation in plan generation (verify file exists)
Document schema location in README.md
Add schema path constants to avoid hardcoding

unit: Test schema loading from canonical path
Verify all commands can load schemas
Test error handling for missing schemas

criteria: All schema references point to canonical location
Schema validation works consistently across commands
No broken schema path errors in plan generation
Documentation clearly states schema location
Grep shows no inconsistent paths

commit.message_template: unify schema paths to canonical location

tasks.title: Implement Schema Validation (Layer 0)
tasks.description: Enforce JSON schema validation for all generated plans as blocking quality gate

modification_points.change: Add schema validation before writing plan.json

implementation: Load learn-plan.schema.json at plan generation time
Implement validation function using JSON schema validator
Validate generated plan.json against schema before writing
Block plan creation if validation fails (throw error)
Provide clear error messages identifying validation failures
Log validation results to session metadata (_validation_log)
Add validation bypass flag for debugging (--skip-validation)

unit: Test with valid plan structure → passes
Test with invalid plan (missing required fields) → fails

criteria: All generated plans conform to schema
Invalid plans are rejected with clear error messages
Schema validation is deterministic and testable
Validation errors identify specific field/constraint violations
Bypass flag works for debugging
verification: Generate plan with missing field → fails with clear error
Check session metadata contains validation log

commit.message_template: implement schema validation gate for plan generation

tasks.title: Implement DAG Cycle Detection (Layer 1)
tasks.description: Validate dependency graph is acyclic before plan creation to prevent impossible learning sequences

modification_points.change: Add DAG cycle detection after schema validation

implementation: Extract dependency graph from knowledge points (id → prerequisites)
Implement DFS-based cycle detection algorithm
Block plan creation if cycles detected (throw error with cycle path)
Generate topological sort for valid DAGs (learning sequence)
Display dependency visualization in plan summary
Add cycle path to error message (e.g., A → B → C → A)
Store topological order in plan metadata

unit: Test acyclic graph → passes, returns topo order
Test cyclic graph → fails, identifies cycle

criteria: Circular dependencies are detected and rejected
Valid DAGs pass validation
Topological order is generated for valid plans
Clear error messages identify cycle locations
Topo order stored in plan for execution guidance
verification: Create plan with cycle → fails with cycle path
Create valid plan → succeeds with topo order

commit.message_template: implement DAG cycle detection for dependency validation

tasks.title: Add Profile-Plan Matching Warnings (Layer 2)
tasks.description: Warn when plan doesn't match user profile proficiency levels to improve personalization

modification_points.change: Add profile-plan matching checks after DAG validation

implementation: Load active profile and extract known_topics with proficiency
Compare plan knowledge points with profile topics
Mark KPs as optional if profile proficiency ≥ 0.8
Warn if plan requires topics not in profile (missing prerequisites)
Generate profile_fingerprint hash for mismatch detection
Allow user to proceed with warnings (non-blocking)
Store matching results in plan metadata (_profile_match)

criteria: High-proficiency topics marked as optional
Missing prerequisites generate warnings
Users can proceed despite warnings
Profile-plan alignment is measurable via metadata
Warnings are informative and actionable
verification: Plan with known topics → some marked optional
Plan with unknown topics → warnings displayed

commit.message_template: add profile-plan matching warnings for personalization

tasks.title: Design Internal CLI State Commands
tasks.description: Design CLI specification for learn state management subcommands to abstract file operations
implementation: Define command interface: ccw learn:write-profile, ccw learn:update-state, ccw learn:read-state
Specify parameters: --profile-id, --data (JSON), --field (for updates)
Define return values: JSON with success/error, data payload
Design error handling: file not found, invalid JSON, schema validation failures
Document atomic write strategy: write to temp file, validate, rename
Define schema validation requirements: validate against profile/state schemas
Add examples for each command
Document security considerations: path traversal prevention

criteria: CLI interface is clearly documented
All state operations have defined commands
Parameters and return values specified
Error handling is documented
Atomic write strategy is documented
Examples provided for each command
verification: Check all CRUD operations are covered

commit.message_template: design internal CLI state management commands

tasks.title: Implement State Management CLI
tasks.description: Implement internal CLI commands for state management with validation and atomic writes
implementation: Implement ccw learn:write-profile with schema validation
Implement ccw learn:update-state with atomic updates (read-modify-write)
Implement ccw learn:read-state with error handling
Add file locking for concurrent access protection (flock or similar)
Implement backup/recovery: backup before write, restore on failure
Write unit tests for all state operations (>80% coverage)
Add integration tests for concurrent access scenarios
Document CLI usage in help text

unit: Test concurrent access with file locking

criteria: All CLI commands work correctly
Schema validation is enforced
Atomic writes prevent corruption
Unit tests achieve >80% coverage
Concurrent access is handled safely
Backup/recovery works correctly
verification: Test concurrent writes → no data loss

tasks.title: Refactor Profile Command to Use CLI
tasks.description: Replace direct file operations with CLI state management calls to improve reliability

modification_points.target: Profile creation and state update sections

implementation: Replace all Read(statePath) with Bash('ccw learn:read-state')
Replace all Write(profilePath, ...) with Bash('ccw learn:write-profile --data ...')
Replace state updates with Bash('ccw learn:update-state --field active_profile_id --value ...')
Update error handling to process CLI return codes and JSON responses
Remove direct file path construction (profilesDir, statePath)
Test backward compatibility with existing profiles
Add migration notes for users with custom profile locations

unit: Test profile creation with CLI → profile file created
Test state update with CLI → state.json updated
Test error handling → CLI errors propagated correctly
integration: Create profile end-to-end → works with CLI

criteria: No direct Read()/Write() calls remain in profile.md
All state operations use CLI commands
Existing profiles continue to work
Error handling is improved with structured errors
Performance is acceptable (CLI overhead minimal)
verification: Grep profile.md for Read\(|Write\( → no matches

commit.message_template: refactor profile command to use state management CLI

tasks.title: Implement Real Agent for Plan Generation
tasks.description: Replace simulated planning with real LLM agent invocation for intelligent, personalized plans

modification_points.change: Create comprehensive prompt with context injection
Replace simulated logic with agent invocation

implementation: Design learn-planning-agent.md prompt with clear instructions and output format
Define agent input context structure: {goal, profile, available_resources, constraints}
Implement context injection: load profile, format as JSON, inject into prompt
Implement agent invocation: Bash('ccw cli -p learn-planning-agent.md --context ...')
Parse structured JSON output from agent (plan with KPs, dependencies, resources)
Implement fallback to template-based planning if agent fails (--no-agent mode)
Add retry logic for agent failures (max 3 retries with exponential backoff)
Validate agent output against schema before accepting

unit: Test agent with various profiles and goals
Test fallback mechanism when agent unavailable
Test retry logic on transient failures

criteria: Agent generates valid plan JSON conforming to schema
Plans are personalized based on user profile data
Fallback mechanism works when agent unavailable
Agent output is validated before acceptance
Retry logic handles transient failures gracefully
verification: Generate plan with agent → personalized KPs
Agent fails → retries then falls back

commit.message_template: implement real agent for intelligent plan generation

tasks.title: Implement JIT Assessment Triggers
tasks.description: Trigger targeted assessments when plan detects low-confidence topics for progressive profiling

modification_points.change: Add confidence check and JIT assessment trigger before agent invocation

implementation: Analyze plan topics against profile confidence scores
Identify topics with confidence < 0.6 (configurable threshold)
Trigger mini-assessment flow via AskUserQuestion for low-confidence topics
Limit assessment to 2-3 questions per topic (brief, targeted)
Update profile with new assessment results via CLI
Regenerate plan with updated profile (or continue with updated context)
Limit to one assessment per topic per session (track in session state)
Add --skip-assessment flag to bypass JIT triggers

unit: Test assessment limit → max once per topic

criteria: Low-confidence topics trigger assessments
Assessments are targeted and brief (2-3 questions)
Profile is updated with new results
Plan reflects updated profile data
User experience is not overly intrusive
Assessment limit prevents repetition
verification: Plan with low-confidence topic → assessment triggered
Check session state tracks assessed topics

commit.message_template: implement JIT assessment triggers for progressive profiling

tasks.title: Streamline Initial Profile Creation
tasks.description: Reduce initial profile creation friction by deferring detailed assessments to JIT triggers

modification_points.change: Make detailed assessments optional, collect only essentials

implementation: Collect only essential fields: experience_level, goal_type, learning_style
Make topic assessment optional (--full-assessment flag for detailed assessment)
Defer topic assessments to JIT triggers during plan generation
Create minimal profile in <1 minute (target: 30-45 seconds)
Mark all topics as low-confidence initially (confidence: 0.3)
Add profile completion percentage indicator
Rely on JIT assessment during plan generation for topic details
Add profile.is_minimal flag to track completion status

unit: Test minimal profile creation → completes quickly
Test full assessment flag → runs detailed assessment
Test profile completion indicator → accurate percentage

criteria: Initial profile creation takes <1 minute
Essential fields are collected
Profile is valid and usable for plan generation
